text: Systematic review of growth mindset and health outcomes. The authors searched PubMed and PsycINFO for English-language studies from 2012 to 2024. Eight studies were included. No meta-analysis was performed. Funding and conflict of interest statements were not reported in the uploaded text.

draft.bottomLine: This review suggests growth mindset may influence some health outcomes, but the evidence base is mixed and not quantitatively pooled.

whatPaperActuallyShows.observedResult: Some included studies reported positive associations, but results were mixed.

keyFindings.finding: Some included studies reported better self-reported outcomes with growth mindset framing.
keyFindings.plainEnglishMeaning: The idea may help in some contexts, but the evidence is not consistent enough to treat it as settled.

evidenceQuality.publicationBiasRisk: Possible because search and inclusion choices may shape which studies appear

limitationsAndGotchas.whyItMatters: The review does not quantify a pooled effect size.
limitationsAndGotchas.practicalConsequence: Readers should not cite this as precise evidence of how large the effect is.
limitationsAndGotchas.whatWouldStrengthenIt: A meta-analysis with risk-of-bias assessment would make the conclusion stronger.
